laboratory_tests: Thyroid function tests (TSH, T3, T4, free T4)
Liver function tests (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, albumin)
Electrolytes (sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, calcium, magnesium, phosphorus)
Iron studies (serum iron, TIBC, transferrin saturation)

abbreviations.PERRLA: pupils equal round reactive to light and accommodation
abbreviations.LOC: loss of consciousness / level of consciousness
abbreviations.HFrEF: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
abbreviations.HFpEF: heart failure with preserved ejection fraction

critical_pairs.distinction: ileum = part of small intestine; ilium = pelvic bone
humerus = upper arm bone; humorous = funny
peroneal = outer lower leg (fibular); perineal = pelvic floor region
ureter = tube from kidney to bladder; urethra = tube from bladder to outside
vesical = relating to bladder; vesicle = small blister or sac
palpation = examining by touch; palpitation = abnormal heartbeat sensation
prostate = male gland; prostrate = lying face down
mucus = the substance (noun); mucous = the membrane (adjective)
perfusion = blood flow to tissue; profusion = abundance
aphagia = inability to swallow; aphasia = inability to speak/understand language
dysphagia = difficulty swallowing; dysphasia = difficulty speaking
dysuria = painful urination; anuria = no urine output
abduction = away from midline; adduction = toward midline
afferent = toward center (sensory); efferent = away from center (motor)
anuresis = absence of urination; enuresis = involuntary urination
menorrhagia = heavy menstrual bleeding; metrorrhagia = irregular bleeding
oral = mouth; aural = ear
callus = bone or skin thickening (noun); callous = emotionally hardened (adjective)
fundi = plural of fundus; fungi = plural of fungus
cirrhosis = liver disease; sclerosis = hardening of tissue
radical = extreme / surgery term; radicle = relating to nerve root
tract = anatomical pathway; tracked = followed
sight = vision; site = location; cite = reference
